summary: Drafted ADR-011 and a bounded DRAFT downstream chain after product-owner root-level rejection of the current map drawing and spatial-index direction. The plan treats ADR-010 only as anti-grid negative proof and proposes a new player-facing terrain-route-node authority where governance footprints are overlays.

tests_run.result: Working tree includes existing lead/product clarification state changes plus this writer package; no forbidden code/content/app paths were modified by this writer.

tests_run.command: node .agents/skills/monsoon-studio-orchestrator/scripts/taskctl.mjs validate M7-STRATEGIC-TERRAIN-RECOVERY-001 && validate M7-STRATEGIC-TERRAIN-SCHEMA-001 && validate M7-STRATEGIC-TERRAIN-FIXTURE-001 && validate M7-STRATEGIC-TERRAIN-RENDERER-INTERACTION-001 && validate M7-STRATEGIC-TERRAIN-VALIDATION-001
tests_run.result: All five strategic terrain task JSON files are VALID.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M7 strategic terrain recovery files
tests_run.result: Recovery task, four downstream tasks, and writer handoff parsed as valid JSON.
Strategic terrain tasks are listed; downstream strategic terrain tasks do not appear READY while recovery remains REVIEW.

decisions.decision: Treat the current map drawing and polygon-first spatial-index direction as rejected, not as a surface needing polish.
ADR-010 remains anti-grid/lattice negative proof but is not the content-lock target architecture.
ADR-011 proposes player-facing authority based on terrain patches, barriers/channels, strategic nodes, route corridors, and district governance overlays.
The schema, fixture, renderer/interaction, and validation tasks are DRAFT and do not authorize implementation until reviewed and started separately.

risks.summary: Replacing the rejected renderer/spatial index affects schema, content, UI, AI reasons, path preview, saves, and validation.
risks.mitigation: Split work into schema, fixture, renderer/interaction, and validation tasks with systems, historical, and QA review gates.

risks.summary: Terrain, port, river, road, pass, and polity claims can become cultural or historical overclaims if fixture work stops being COMPOSITE.
risks.mitigation: Fixture task routes real-world claims to historical_researcher and requires HISTORICAL/INFERRED/COMPOSITE/FICTIONAL tagging.

requested_action: Review ADR-011 and the DRAFT downstream task chain. Do not approve M7 content lock or downstream implementation from this writer handoff alone.
